items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom

items.summary: Mr Lees will face a Supreme Court trial for allegedly killing Meaghan Louise Rose, whose death was originally suspected to be a suicide.

items.summary: Police allege a drone was used to deliver meth in Wollongong before it crashed, leading to a wider investigation.

items.summary: At first glance, these findings sound alarming, but before we draw big conclusions about worsening drug use, it's worth taking a closer look at what this data can and can't tell us.

items.title: Blues out to rewrite Women's State of Origin history against Maroons in Newcastle series opener
items.summary: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

items.title: Police say 'timeframe of survivability' ending for missing 5yo Sharon
items.summary: After speaking to survival experts, NT police says the "timeframe of survivability" is coming to an end for a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs. The police commissioner also confirmed the search had been complicated by the fact the child is non-verbal.

items.summary: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

items.summary: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.summary: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
items.summary: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

items.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
items.summary: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

items.summary: A former NSW police officer has failed in an attempt to overturn a conviction for using unreasonable force in the arrest of two teenage boys.

items.summary: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

items.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition

items.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape

items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

items.title: New South Wales still committed to saving Tomago Aluminium as negotiations stall
items.summary: The NSW government remains committed to Tomago Aluminium but is still waiting to learn how much the bailout will cost.

items.title: Northern Beaches Hospital returns to public ownership after death of Joe Massa
items.summary: The NSW premier says a family's campaigning after their toddler died following treatment at Northern Beaches Hospital helped change public policy.

items.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
items.summary: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
items.summary: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

items.title: Mother who faked son's eye cancer diagnosis to raise money sentenced to over four years' jail
items.summary: A mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to raise thousands of dollars has been jailed for more than four years for the "cruel, calculated" and "manipulative" crime.

items.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
items.summary: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

items.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted in probe over seashells photo
items.summary: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

items.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
items.summary: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.
